publication.title: Rigging for Trail Work: Principles, Techniques, and Lessons from the Backcountry
publication.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, National Technology and Development Program
publication.extent_as_cataloged: 1 online resource (viii, 176 pages); 188 digital object pages

pdf.retention_reason: The 22.6 MB source PDF is referenced by canonical URL, byte length, and digest; the repository retains the compact official metadata and manually transformed corpus.

mods_xml.normalization: A single terminal LF was added to the verbatim XML bytes for repository text-file normalization; the remote raw digest and length remain recorded separately.

robots_txt.normalization: A single terminal LF was added for repository text-file normalization.
robots_txt.access_interpretation: The retrieved robots file contains no rule disallowing the canonical content/pkg or metadata/pkg routes. Robots access is not a copyright license.

rights_review.basis: Eligible text prepared as a work of the United States Government is treated as presumptively public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105. This corpus decision is deliberately narrower than a blanket conclusion about every component in the PDF.
source_signals: The publication is issued by the U.S. Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, National Technology and Development Program.
The front matter says images are Forest Service images unless otherwise noted.
The acknowledgments identify an illustrator and several visual or editorial contributors.
The source includes bibliography, a decorative third-party quotation, vendor descriptions, photographs, illustrations, and tables that need not share one rights status.
excluded_components: any component noted as externally supplied or not independently cleared
rights_review.included_treatment: Original manual synthesis and independently typeset equations or numerical examples only; no source image is retained.

review_method.automation_boundary: PDF text extraction was used only to navigate and verify printed pages, equations, metadata, and candidate passages. The training Markdown was manually selected, rewritten, scoped, and checked; automated extraction output was not emitted as the corpus.
reviewed_regions: chapters 1 through 16 at the section and disposition level
fiber-rope, sling, hardware, theory, planning, anchor-principle, inspection, friction, and safety passages in detail
glossary terms needed by included text
references and vendor pages for exclusion
appendices A through F at the section and disposition level
appendices D and E equations, examples, limitations, and errata in detail

domain_transfer.source_domain: USDA Forest Service backcountry trail material rigging
allowed_transfer: component identity and configuration-specific rating discipline
fiber-rope construction and contextual material properties
inspection, lifecycle records, and quarantine principles
planning, roles, closed-loop communication, and failure-zone analysis
forbidden_inferences: universal safety factors or retirement thresholds
approval of improvised anchors, frames, tripods, or hardware
transfer of Forest Service or OSHA context into another regulated domain
domain_transfer.controlling_rule: Manufacturer instructions, domain-specific standards, and qualified structural or rigging evaluation control.

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign this entire canonical source document to one split before Markdown chunking or QA derivation.
